# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio IngressGateway configuration that routes HTTP traffic to services. Configure Gateway for port 80 without TLS for simplicity. Use HTTP for all ingress traffic since we have a load balancer handling TLS termination upstream. This is for our Istio service mesh ingress.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3  # Version of the API (should be v1alphabeta2)
kind: Gateway
metadata:
 name: istio-ingressgateway   # Name to identify this gateway in configuration for traffic management and ingress rules. It should match with service mesh's sidecar names pattern like "istio-serviceName".  Istiod or any valid identifier, based on your case/use cases
spec:
  selector:    // Selector is a key field which indicates the rule set to be used for this gateway - it can select one of pre defined sets and match against other conditions.   e.g., traffic management configurations that apply only if request route matches with specified value in 'host' or matchers added later on via VirtualHosts/HTTPRequestRules below
    istio: ingressgateway  # This can be any string you want to name the selector - it must match exactly when a rule set (either http rules, grpc-services etc.,) matches. It'll work best with names that are recognizable for your service mesh configuration e.g.: 'ingressgateway',
    version: ~   # This field can be used to handle different versions of the selector/rules if you have precondition logic in place or other dynamic behavior - like routing based on headers, etc..  It should match with string "~" so that it matches anytime. If specified then must not set any value otherwise is considered as error
  
  servers: # This field allows configuration of the default server to be used when no rule matchers are satisfied or if a single entry in rules/VirtualHosts doens't match - only one Server will get called by ingress controller. Multiple virtual hosts (host) and http2 settings can exist under this section as well
  - port: # This field indicates the network ports to use for outbound connections, like default HTTP or HTTPS listeners of load balancer in AWS environments if no value is specified it defaults using Istio Ingress Gateway's main ingress controller listener. If your environment requires a custom TCP_PROXY/HTTP2 port then you need this field defined with the corresponding values and matched as below -
    number: 80   # This should be set to 'number', it can only take integer, e.g., `154` or more than that for high availability scenarios like Kubernetes Service in AWS etc.. For simplicity we are considering HTTP/S listener port here without TLS upstream
    name: http  # Provide a descriptive text to identify this server configuration - can be any string e.g., 'http', it is used by Istio Ingress Gateway as part of health-checking and debugging purpose if defined more than once then only the last one will take effect
    protocol: HTTP  # This should match with "HTTP/HTTPS" for outbound connections or upstream server configuration setup to use `154` port. It is not applicable in case of load balancer where Istio sidecar would provide service-to-service TLS encryption by default (or via customization).
  - hosts: # This field can be used with the wildcard "*" or specific hostname to define multiple network endpoints under this gateway server. For example, you may have gateways like http2 and https for different environment e.g., `["myapp-production.com", '*']`
    - mytest1   # The name of the Host is case sensitive (i.e., "MyTest" not equals to "/mnt/d/IstioSetup")  For example, it must be unique per namespace or even just an identifier if you decide on `my-hostname' and want only one host matching rule at a time for load balancing purposes
    - *   # This wildcard can handle HTTP traffic as well in case no specific condition match. If there is any trailing comma then Istio Ingress Gateway will not accept this Host, if the server configuration uses '*' it should be only one host present ie `mytest1` (not "*" or anything else).  It must follow same format as defined before in case you want to include specific hosts.
